raw_consumer_statement: Today is XX/XX/. Since XXXX XXXX, I have been trying to get Citibank to refund three clearly fraudulent charges on my account to no avail. I noticed three suspicious charges on my account. I called to report them, and Citibank promptly issued me a new card. I thought the matter was closed until I received an email on XXXX XXXX stating that they believe the charges to be legitimate based on evidence provided by the merchant. I called again and asked if they could send me a copy of the evidence they were provided, and I was told I'd have it in my email in 24 hours. By the next evening, I had not seen anything, so I called again. I was again told I would have it in 24 hours. I logged into my account and found that I could pull up the evidence myself in their dispute center. Not once did a representative suggest that as an option. I downloaded the pdf documents ( please see attached ) and quickly realized that the " evidence '' was flimsy at best. 1 ) The web address the merchant provided does not exist : XXXX XXXX XXXX. 2 ) I am not associated with the email address the merchant claims I used, XXXX. The irony of " lost XXXX '' is not lost on me. 3 ) the merchant 's IP address originates in XXXX XXXX, XXXX, XXXX. Additionally, Citibank told me that my credit card was not physically present at the time of purchase, and that they agreed the discrepancies I provided were compelling. All this clearly spells out fraud to me, but here it is three months later and I'm having to fight to get any sort of movement out of Citibank. My last interaction was this evening... I told them they could have until end of the next billing cycle before I submitted a complaint here, but I've subsequently changed my mind. This is ridiculous.
